title: hieracium planchonianum subsp. planchonianum
intro: Hieracium planchonianum subsp. planchonianum, also known as Planch's hawkweed, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium planchonianum subsp. planchonianum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as arthritis and skin issues. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are applied topically to the skin or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
